ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT TODD SHIPYARDS CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE 22 (SOPHISTICATED USER) AND AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE 15 (STATE OF SCIENTIFIC KNOWLEDGE) AND TAKEN OFF CALENDAR AS TO STRICT LIABILITY. ASSUMING WITHOUT DECIDING THAT SOME VARIATION OF THE "SOPHISTICATED USER" DEFENSE CAN BE ASSERTED BY A PREMISES OWNER/CONTRACTOR WHO IS SUED BASED ON AN ALLEGATION THAT IT DISTURBED ASBESTOS RATHER THAN SUPPLYING AN ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCT, DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUBMIT EVIDENCE SUFFICIENT TO SUCCESSFULLY INVOKE SUCH A DEFENSE. AMONG OTHER THINGS, DEFENDANT FAILED TO ESTABLISH THAT DEFENDANT ENSURED THAT PLAINTIFF, EITHER DIRECTLY OR THROUGH HIS EMPLOYER, WAS INFORMED ABOUT THE HAZARDS OF THE ASBESTOS-DISTURBING ACTIVITIES CONDUCTED BY DEFENDANT THAT WERE KNOWN OR SHOULD HAVE BEEN KNOWN TO DEFENDANT AND THAT PLAINTIFF KNEW OR SHOULD HAVE KNOWN OF THE HAZARDS OF THE ASBESTOS-DISTURBING ACTIVITIES CONDUCTED BY DEFENDANT. THE DECLARATION OF DR. RICHARD COHEN CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER DEFENDANT SHOULD HAVE KNOWN OF THE HAZARDS OF ASBESTOS DURING THE RELEVANT TIME PERIOD. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO DR. COHEN'S DECLARATION ARE OVERRULED. PLAINITFF REPRESENTS THAT HE DISMISSED HIS CLAIM FOR STRICT LIABILITY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077

Off Calendar Jan-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Andrew K. Murphy, Esq., of YARON & ASSOCIATES (415-658-2929), appearing for defendant / moving party Todd Shipyards Corporation
Jennifer Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff
/ opposing party James Feduska

RULING:
DEFENDANT TODD SHIPYARDS CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Todd Shipyards Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to affirmative defense 22 (sophisticated user) and affirmative defense 15 (state of scientific knowledge) and taken off calendar as to strict liability. Assuming without deciding that some variation of the "sophisticated user" defense can be asserted by a premises owner/contractor who is sued based on an allegation that it disturbed asbestos rather than supplying an asbestos-containing product, defendant failed to submit evidence sufficient to successfully invoke such a defense. Among other things, defendant failed to establish that defendant ensured that plaintiff, either directly or through his employer, was informed about the hazards of the asbestos-disturbing activities conducted by defendant that were known or should have been known to defendant and that plaintiff knew or should have known of the hazards of the asbestos-disturbing activities conducted by defendant. The declaration of Dr. Richard Cohen creates a triable issue whether defendant should have known of the hazards of asbestos during the relevant time period. Defendant's objections to Dr. Cohen's declaration are overruled. Plainitff represents that he dismissed his claim for strict liability. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
